weather_line: Low cloud in the hollows · briefcase conditions: luminous

stories.headline: Gringotts Formally Requests Briefcase Consultation; Goblins Cite Statute 7 of the Luminous Unattended Items Accord; Christine Is Called In Before Her First Cup of Tea
body: In a development that surprised Ministry investigators, delighted no one, and made the duty goblin at Gringotts look briefly vindicated, the bank this morning issued a formal consultation request under Statute 7 of the Luminous Unattended Items Accord — a treaty negotiated in 1842, invoked twice since, and until today considered decorative. The briefcase, still glowing in the back room of Weasleys' Wizard Wheezes, apparently falls within its jurisdiction. The goblins have opinions. They have had them since Friday.

Christine arrived at Gringotts at seven fifty-one and was handed the consultation file before she had removed her cloak. She read it in four minutes, asked two questions the Ministry had not thought to ask, and identified a procedural irregularity in the Accord's third sub-clause that the goblins acknowledged with what observers described as 'a nod that contained some respect.' The briefcase has not been opened. Christine has not yet decided whether it needs to be. This is, most agree, the correct place to start.

stories.headline: The Kitchen Table, Now Clear for the First Time in a Fortnight, Raises a Question About What Comes Next; the Jeep Has Formed a View
body: The southwest quadrant chart was folded on Sunday evening and returned to its place on the shelf, where it sits beside the November Hempworth survey and two brass ducks facing a direction that has not yet been named. The kitchen table is bare. This has not happened since before the conditional notation was identified, and the household is adjusting to it with the particular combination of satisfaction and mild restlessness that follows a good resolution.

Steve has not unfolded anything new. He has, however, been outside twice to look at the Jeep from a specific angle. The Jeep, for its part, was washed on Saturday and has been parked facing northeast since Sunday afternoon. This is not the direction of the southwest quadrant. It may not be the direction of anything yet. The brass ducks have not commented.

stories.headline: Monday Morning Vacancy Assessed, Claimed, and Defended; Property Dispute Reaches Early Resolution; One Party Maintains She Was There First and Always Has Been
body: The warm spot on the left end of the sofa — vacated at seven forty-nine when Christine left for Gringotts — was assessed, claimed, and occupied by eight-oh-one. A counter-claim was registered at eight-oh-four by a party who arrived from the direction of the kitchen and stated, without preamble, that she had been here first and this was a matter of historical record. The dispute was resolved by eight-oh-seven. The original occupant held the position. The challenger accepted the adjacent armchair under protest and has since revised her assessment of it upward.
Lucy has been seen near the biscuit tin twice and near the evidence of same exactly zero times. This is consistent with all prior reports.

stories.headline: Ministry Issues Monday Briefing Confirming the Week Has Begun; Declines to Elaborate; Attaches a Map of the Diagon Alley Back-Room in Question That Does Not Include the Back Room
body: The Ministry of Magic's Monday briefing, released at nine-fifteen, confirmed that the week had begun, that the Luminous Unattended Items Accord consultation was 'proceeding through appropriate channels,' and that the man in the suit — last logged entering the Weasleys' premises on Thursday at six-forty-two — remained 'of ongoing relevance to enquiries.' A map was attached. It depicted Diagon Alley in helpful detail and omitted the back room entirely. This is either an oversight or a position. The Ministry has not clarified which.

The glowing briefcase, reached for comment via Extendable Ear, did not respond. It did, however, glow slightly more brightly for approximately four seconds, which the Ministry's statement described as 'within normal parameters' and which everyone who was present described as not that.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Was nowhere near the biscuit tin this morning, has never been near the biscuit tin, and finds the entire line of enquiry frankly exhausting. Two biscuits are unaccounted for. These facts are unrelated.

Ellie: Spent twenty-five minutes this morning making friends with the window reflection, then went to look for it on the other side of the glass, and is now resting to recover from the philosophical implications.
Jersey: Secured the left sofa position by eight-oh-one, defended it by eight-oh-seven, and has not moved since except to adjust to a slightly warmer angle. Considers the morning a complete success.
founding_editor_note: That briefcase back room wants proper shelving — floating all that luminous weight on a single joist is how you get subsidence, and then nobody can find anything. I'd have put in a bracket. — D.

in_brief.sport: Monday Weightlifting Report: the consultation file was lifted, read, and set down with authority; judges award full marks for form.
in_brief.forecast: Partly overcast with a high chance of procedural irregularities; luminous conditions persist through Wednesday; brass ducks facing northeast by nightfall.

storyline_updates.title: The Briefcase Returns
storyline_updates.summary: Day 3: Gringotts invoked Statute 7 of the Luminous Unattended Items Accord and formally requested Christine's consultation. She identified a procedural irregularity in the Accord's third sub-clause; the goblins acknowledged it. The briefcase remains unopened in the Weasleys' back room. The man in the suit is still unaccounted for. The Ministry's map omitted the back room. Day 4 should resolve the arc: either the briefcase is opened — with Christine present, under the Accord's terms — and what is inside either explains everything or explains nothing in a satisfying way; or the man in the suit reappears to collect it, and Christine's irregularity either helps or becomes irrelevant in a way she finds professionally interesting. The Pulp Fiction glowing-contents tradition should be honored: whatever is inside illuminates the room, satisfies the observer, and is not described.

storyline_updates.title: Northeast by Nightfall
storyline_updates.summary: New arc opening quietly: the Jeep has been parked facing northeast since Sunday afternoon. Steve has looked at it twice from a specific angle. No chart has been unfolded. The brass ducks are still facing southwest from the shelf. Something new is forming — a direction, a notation, a route not yet named. Day 2 should surface the first material evidence: a new notation, a map edge, or a name spoken aloud for the first time.
